survivors_flagged.one_line: Live Zijin all-cash arb at C$44 (~21% spread, Outside Date Jul 29 2026, China/Africa approvals pending) plus Kurmuk first-gold pour mid-2026 — two dated catalysts, real deal-break asymmetry.

Enact: controlling holder Genworth (~81%) is methodically selling down its stake into a dated $500M Enact repurchase agreement — own-the-suppressed-underlying overhang-clearing with capital return, but a slow grind with no single hardening trigger.

ADC Therapeutics restructured (June 2026, -17% staff, runway to 2028) around ZYNLONTA with a dated Aug 2026 pre-sBLA FDA meeting and Q4 2026 sBLA submission — real dated milestones but a ~$1 micro-cap regulatory grind, not high-conviction asymmetry.
Announced Jun-15-26 plan to separate MRD vs Immune Medicine; preferred separation path by year-end 2026 — dated forward spin/structural-alts.
Signed $15.00 cash take-private by GIP/EQT; holders approved Jun-26-26, pending US+foreign regulatory clearances, close late-26/early-27 — live arb spread.
Aimco Plan of Sale & Liquidation approved by holders 6-Feb-2026; serial liquidating distributions underway, $620M+ assets under contract — dated wind-down, own the underlying as cash returns.
Pivotal ALPHA3 cema-cel CAR-T: DSMB interim EFS analysis 1H-2026 + primary EFS readout 2H-2026 (BLA 2027) — dated binaries for a clinical-stage name with asymmetric setup.
Multiple dated 2026 FDA decisions: AVT16 (Entyvio) BLA accepted Jun-8, AVT05/AVT06 resubmitted Jun-4 on 6-month review; stacked biosimilar approval calendar.
Pivotal Phase 3 LUCIDITY topline for avexitide in post-bariatric hypoglycemia anticipated Q3-2026; clean dated binary, Breakthrough designation, first-in-class PBH.

verified.catalyst: Zijin Gold's binding C$44.00/share (~US$31.5) all-cash takeover of Allied Gold — shareholder-approved, Canada + African competition cleared, with the SOLE remaining gate being China's NDRC, plus a second dated catalyst in the Kurmuk (Ethiopia) first-gold pour due mid-2026.
verified.dated_milestone: July 29, 2026 outside date (extended once from May 29; further extension to ~Sept 29, 2026 explicitly possible) — gated on China NDRC sign-off. Secondary: Kurmuk first-gold pour mid-2026.
verified.edge: M — wide ~38% spread to the cash price at a live US$22.77 is real asymmetry, but the binding gate is the harder China NDRC (actively questioning the premium + Mali political risk after gold corrected ~18% from the ~US$5,500 deal-signing peak), and the stock now sits AT the ~US$23 standalone floor, so the market already discounts a meaningful break/slip probability; not a clean H.
verified.primary_source: GlobeNewswire 2026-05-29 (Allied Gold outside-date extension to Jul 29, 2026); InvestingNews/Mining.com June 2026 (China NDRC delay, questioning premium + Mali risk); Allied Gold IR / Mining Review 2026 (Kurmuk first gold mid-2026); FMP live quote 2026-06-29 US$22.77.
verified.kill_fact: Zijin Gold's C$44.00/share all-cash takeover of Allied Gold is shareholder-approved (Mar 31 mtg) with Canada (national-security review cleared Apr 9, 2026) and African regulators done; deal is NOT closed/NOT fired — sole remaining gate is China's NDRC, which has actively raised the ~27% premium and Mali-security risk, pushing the outside date to July 29, 2026 (already extended once; possible ~Sept 29 further extension). Live AAUC US$22.755 (NYSE, 2026-06-29) sits right at the ~US$23 standalone floor (yearLow 11.40, 200d avg 25.12), leaving a ~38% spread to the ~US$31.5 cash price and near-zero downside cushion — the tape prices a genuine break/slip probability. Second dated catalyst confirmed: Kurmuk (Ethiopia) first gold pour on track for mid-2026 (Q2/H1), ~175koz partial-year 2026. CORRECTION: dossier's emphasis on 'advanced-stage African host-country approvals' as the binding gate is wrong — NDRC is the binding gate; African/Canadian clearances are already done. Score trimmed to 7: dated asymmetry is real but the harder Chinese regulatory gate plus zero downside cushion at the floor warrant lower conviction than the dossier's 8.
verified.analysis: VERIFIED LIVE and FORWARD: Zijin's C$44 cash takeover is shareholder-approved with Canada + ECOWAS/COMESA cleared; deal is NOT closed and NOT fired, outside date July 29, 2026 (already extended once, with a further ~Sept 29 extension flagged as possible). MATERIAL CORRECTION to the dossier: the binding gate is China's NDRC (not the "advanced-stage African host-country approvals" the dossier emphasized) — NDRC is actively questioning the premium and Mali political risk after gold fell ~18% from the ~US$5,500 deal-signing peak, a real and currently-unpriced regulatory risk. At a live US$22.77 the spread to the ~US$31.5 cash price is ~38%, but the stock now sits at/below the ~US$23 standalone floor (vs the dossier's $25.26 working live), confirming the market discounts a genuine break/slip probability; the Kurmuk mid-2026 first-gold pour adds a second dated forward catalyst. Holding ACTIVE at score 7 (down from dossier's 8): asymmetry is real and dated, but the harder Chinese gate plus a near-zero downside cushion at today's price warrant trimming conviction.

verified.catalyst: Enact's $500M buyback (authorized Feb 2026) under which Genworth sells pro-rata to MAINTAIN — not reduce — its ~81% stake, so no overhang is actually being cleared.
verified.dated_milestone: 2026 capital-return guidance of ~$500M (reaffirmed Q1 2026 call 2026-05-06); next milestone Q2 2026 earnings ~early Aug 2026 — but it is a recurring run-rate, not a dated de-risking event.
verified.bloom_gates: None pass cleanly. G1 (named counterparty Genworth) and G2 (Feb 2, 2026 Share Repurchase Agreement) exist on paper, but both fail the FORWARD/UNPRICED test: the agreement is explicitly designed to KEEP Genworth at ~81%, so it is not a stake-overhang reduction; G3 fails — the ~$500M figure is reaffirmed public guidance, fully re-rated, no specific unpriced figure.
verified.edge: L — the premise (Genworth "selling down" to clear an overhang) is factually inverted: filings show Genworth held ~81% in Q4'25 and still ~81% (113.4M shares) after the latest sales; the buyback maintains its percentage. No mispricing edge; story is known and priced.
verified.primary_source: Enact Q1 2026 earnings call/transcript reaffirming ~$500M 2026 capital return and ~81% Genworth ownership (2026-05-06); Genworth Form 4 sales under Feb 2, 2026 Share Repurchase Agreement (filed 3/31 and 5/29 2026).
verified.analysis: The flagged thesis misreads the mechanics: Genworth's repeated Form 4 sales (820K @ $40.92 on 3/31, 602K @ $42.91 on 5/29) are pro-rata participations in Enact's own $500M buyback under the Feb 2, 2026 Share Repurchase Agreement, structured to HOLD Genworth's stake at ~81%, not to grind it down — so there is no overhang being cleared and no single hardening trigger. The capital-return figure (~$500M for 2026, dividend raised to $0.24) was reaffirmed on the 2026-05-06 Q1 call and is a recurring, fully-priced run-rate, not a dated forward de-rerating catalyst. It fails the strict gate on FORWARD/UNPRICED (already fired/re-rated) and on a real specific overhang-clearing commitment; verdict NONE.

verified.catalyst: August 2026 pre-sBLA FDA meeting to set the regulatory path for ZYNLONTA + rituximab in 2L+ r/r DLBCL, gating a Q4 2026 sBLA submission off the stat-sig LOTIS-5 Phase 3 readout.
verified.dated_milestone: Pre-sBLA FDA meeting — August 2026 (next date); sBLA submission targeted Q4 2026
verified.bloom_gates: G2 — concrete, company-committed dated regulatory action (Aug 2026 pre-sBLA meeting -> Q4 2026 sBLA filing); G1/G3 not cleanly met (no named external counterparty deal, no specific currently-unpriced sales/value figure disclosed)
verified.edge: L — the gating LOTIS-5 PFS data already fired June 3, 2026 (HR 0.73, p=0.008) and is partly re-rated; the Aug meeting is a non-public, non-decisional path-setting meeting, not a PDUFA/approval; a serious safety signal (Grade 5 AEs 13.2% vs 4.6%, 25.5% discontinuations, deaths concentrated in 75+) clouds the benefit-risk the FDA will probe; micro-cap regulatory grind with loan-covenant liquidity caveat on the into-2028 runway.
verified.primary_source: ADC Therapeutics PRNewswire reorg release (Jun 24, 2026) + StockTitan LOTIS-5 Phase 3 results (Jun 3, 2026); FMP quote (Jun 29, 2026)
verified.analysis: Facts are well-corroborated: a June 24, 2026 ZYNLONTA-focused reorg (17% staff cut, ~$10M annual savings, ~$3M Q2 charge) layered on a June 3, 2026 LOTIS-5 Phase 3 confirmatory win (mPFS 6.1 vs 4.7 mo) sets up a dated Aug 2026 pre-sBLA FDA meeting and a Q4 2026 sBLA filing. This is a genuine dated forward catalyst (G2), but it is a non-decisional procedural meeting, not a binary approval, and the asymmetry is weak: the value-driving data already printed, an elevated Grade 5/discontinuation safety signal threatens the benefit-risk the FDA must accept, and this is a sub-$130M micro-cap leaning on a covenant-constrained runway. Real and dated, but low-conviction — WATCH, not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Adaptive Biotechnologies plans to separate its profitable MRD diagnostics business (RemainCo) from Immune Medicine (sale/spin/partnership), with the preferred path to be identified by year-end 2026 — a sum-of-the-parts unlock.
verified.dated_milestone: Preferred separation path to be identified by year-end 2026 (next date: Dec 31, 2026)
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (specific currently-priced-as-conglomerate figures disclosed: MRD 2025 rev $212M, +$15M adj. EBITDA, profitable; Immune Medicine to exit separately) and a structural-action G2 concrete commitment to separate
verified.edge: M-L: real dated forward structural catalyst, but the stock has already re-rated hard on the Jun-15 news — $20.94 vs $15.3 50dma (+37%), sitting at the 52-wk high $21.31 (yr low $9.96). The easy SoP gap is largely priced; only the path-decision/execution optionality (YE26→2027) remains un-fired.
verified.primary_source: Adaptive Biotechnologies press release / SEC 8-K, "Announces Plan to Separate Its MRD and Immune Medicine Businesses," Jun-15-2026 (globenewswire.com; sec.gov EDGAR CIK 0001478320)
verified.analysis: Confirmed: on Jun-15-2026 ADPT announced intent to separate MRD (which stays as Adaptive, RemainCo) from Immune Medicine, evaluating sale/separation/partnership/licensing, with the preferred path to be identified by year-end 2026 (alongside an upsized $300M convertible offering). It is a genuine dated, forward, company-specific structural catalyst — not a macro bet — and the announced leg has not yet been executed. The gating issue is re-rating: shares are at the 52-week high and ~37% above the 50-day average, so the conglomerate-discount unlock is substantially priced in already; residual edge is the path-decision and execution optionality into 2027, hence WATCH rather than ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Signed $15.00/sh all-cash take-private by GIP/BlackRock + EQT consortium (with CalPERS & QIA); stockholders approved Jun-26-26 and HSR cleared, now pending remaining FERC/PUCO/NYPSC/CFIUS and foreign regulatory clearances before close late-26/early-27.
verified.dated_milestone: Next: remaining regulatory clearances (FERC/PUCO/NYPSC/CFIUS + foreign) gating deal close, expected late-2026/early-2027.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named acquirers GIP/EQT/CalPERS/QIA), G2 (concrete $15.00/sh cash commitment), G3 (specific currently-unpriced ~2.4% gross spread to deal price)
verified.edge: L — only ~2.4% gross spread (14.645 vs 15.00) over a 6-12mo+ horizon to close; deal-spread economics are thin and the remaining gates (CFIUS + foreign regulators) carry real timeline/extension risk, so annualized arb IRR is modest and any deal-break downside (~$10-11 unaffected) dwarfs the upside.
verified.primary_source: PRNewswire / AES 8-K via StockTitan, "AES Stockholders Approve Acquisition..." (Jun-26-26); HSR expiry Jun-22-26 per DEFM14A.
verified.analysis: This is a real, dated, forward merger-arb: a signed definitive agreement at $15.00 cash, stockholder approval secured Jun-26-26, HSR period expired Jun-22-26, with the genuinely unfired leg being remaining utility/FERC/CFIUS/foreign approvals gating a close in late-2026/early-2027 — AES trades at $14.645, leaving ~2.4% to the deal price. It passes all three Bloom gates (named counterparties, concrete cash figure, specific unpriced spread) and is forward/not fully re-rated, but the edge is low: the spread is narrow, the timeline long, and the regulatory complexity (CFIUS plus multiple foreign jurisdictions) keeps break/extension risk live against a large deal-break downside. WATCH rather than ACTIVE because the risk-reward of capturing a sub-3% spread over a multi-quarter regulatory slog is unattractive on a standalone basis.

verified.catalyst: Holder-approved Plan of Sale & Liquidation is underway with serial liquidating distributions; ~$3.00–$4.35/share of an expected $5.75–$7.10/share aggregate still to be returned vs a $2.97 price as remaining apartment assets are sold for cash.
verified.dated_milestone: Three additional properties under contract expected to close Q2 2026 (by ~2026-06-30), funding the next liquidating distribution; subsequent distributions event-driven as remaining assets sell.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparties: LaTerra Capital/Respark $455M Chicago close 3/27; four buyers on 12 properties ~$680M w/ $30M non-refundable deposits), G2 (board-approved Plan of Sale, holder vote 2/6/2026; serial declared distributions), G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: ~$3.00–$4.35/sh remaining of $5.75–$7.10 aggregate vs $2.97)
verified.edge: M — only $2.75 of $5.75–$7.10/sh paid so the majority of value is still forward, and midpoint remaining (~$3.68) is ~24% above the $2.97 price; capped because distributions are asset-sale-timed (no fixed calendar) and the un-contracted asset tail carries apartment cap-rate/financing risk, with the low end ($3.00) barely above price.
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts independently confirmed vs primary sources (Aimco PRNewswire/StockTitan, 6/28/26 quote): holder approval 2/6/2026; $1.45 (paid 3/13) + $1.30 (paid 6/3) = $2.75/sh declared; aggregate $5.75-$7.10/sh net estimate; $455M 7-property Chicago portfolio to LaTerra/Respark closed 3/27/2026; 12 properties ~$680M under contract across four buyers w/ ~$30M non-refundable deposits; ten properties sold YTD 2026; price $2.97. Remaining math holds: $5.75-$7.10 - $2.75 = $3.00-$4.35/sh still forward, largely cash/contracted-backed. FIRED-not-forward attack fails (paid $2.75 correctly excluded). Caveats accurate: event-driven timing (no fixed next date), unsold-asset cap-rate tail, lower-bound $3.00 vs $2.97 = thin cushion. Tradeable common (AIV-A preferred is separate, not the instrument). Real liquidation-arb spread, not priced/story.
verified.analysis: Aimco's liquidation is real, holder-approved (2/6/2026), and partially de-risked: ten properties sold in 2026 including a $455M Chicago portfolio (LaTerra/Respark, closed 3/27) and 12 more under contract (~$680M, $30M non-refundable deposits). With $2.75/share already declared ($1.45 Feb + $1.30 Apr, paid 6/3) against a $5.75–$7.10 aggregate target, roughly $3.00–$4.35/share remains to be distributed versus a $2.97 share price — a recovery spread that is still forward, not yet re-rated, and largely backed by contracted/cash proceeds rather than a macro view. Main caveats: distributions are event-driven (no fixed next date) and the unsold-asset tail introduces cap-rate sensitivity, so the lower bound offers little cushion.

verified.catalyst: Pivotal ALPHA3 cema-cel (allogeneic CAR-T) in 1L-consolidation LBCL: independent DSMB-monitored interim event-free-survival (EFS) analysis is the next dated readout, with primary EFS to follow.
verified.dated_milestone: Interim EFS analysis ~mid-2027; primary EFS ~mid-2028; ~220-pt enrollment target by YE-2027 (Allogene guidance, Apr 13 2026)
verified.bloom_gates: G3 partial — interim EFS is a specific, currently-unpriced clinical figure with a date, but distant (~mid-2027), not the near-dated 2H-2026 binary the flag asserts
verified.edge: L — flag premise is stale/wrong: the "1H-2026 DSMB interim EFS" already occurred as the Apr 13 2026 interim futility analysis (58.3% vs 16.7% MRD clearance, trial continued); no durable re-rating (px near 200d avg $1.78, far below yr-high $4.46), and the real next binary slips to ~mid-2027.
verified.primary_source: Allogene IR / GlobeNewswire press release "Interim Futility Analysis from Pivotal ALPHA3 Trial" (Apr 13, 2026); BioSpace coverage same date
verified.analysis: The triage flag's dated binaries are factually broken: the DSMB interim EFS the flag put in "1H-2026" was reported April 13, 2026 as the interim futility analysis (cema-cel 58.3% vs observation 16.7% MRD clearance, clean safety, trial continues), and Allogene's own guidance pushes the interim EFS to ~mid-2027 and primary EFS to ~mid-2028 — not 2H-2026 primary / BLA-2027. So the near-dated asymmetric setup the flag priced has already fired/mis-dated; what remains is a genuine but ~12+ month-out micro-cap clinical binary. Real and dated, but distant and not the unpriced near-term catalyst claimed — demote to WATCH, score 3.

verified.catalyst: Stacked FDA biosimilar decisions: AVT05 (Simponi/golimumab) and AVT06 (Eylea/aflibercept) BLAs resubmitted on a 6-month review clock after Alvotech resolved its Reykjavik facility Post-Action Letter, plus AVT16 (Entyvio) BLA accepted — each a Teva-commercialized approval gate.
verified.dated_milestone: AVT05/AVT06 FDA action ~Dec-2026 (six-month review of Jun-4-2026 resubmission); AVT16 (Entyvio) action ~mid-2027 (standard BLA review of Jun-8-2026 acceptance)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: FDA decisions + Teva commercial partner) + G2 (concrete commitment: BLAs resubmitted/accepted on defined review clocks)
verified.edge: M — the AVT05/AVT06 resubmission narrows a known overhang (the prior CRL/Post-Action Letter on the Reykjavik plant) to a hard ~Dec-2026 binary; manufacturing-remediation outcome is genuinely uncertain so not fully priced, but the approval path itself is well-telegraphed and biosimilar approvals are incremental rather than transformational, capping the surprise magnitude.
verified.primary_source: Alvotech press releases via GlobeNewswire — resubmission Jun-4-2026 (AVT05/AVT06) and AVT16 BLA acceptance Jun-8-2026
verified.kill_fact: All three milestones verified against primary sources (Alvotech press releases / SEC 6-K / Pearce IP): AVT16 (Entyvio/vedolizumab) interchangeable-biosimilar BLA accepted Jun-8-2026 (first accepted vedolizumab biosimilar); AVT05 (Simponi/Simponi Aria = golimumab) and AVT06 (Eylea/aflibercept) BLAs resubmitted Jun-4-2026 on an expected six-month FDA review after Alvotech responded to the Reykjavik facility Post-Action Letter and May-2026 cGMP inspection observations, pointing to ~Dec-2026 action. Teva commercializes all three. Catalysts are forward/un-fired, dated binaries, tradeable in ALVO common. CORRECTION: triage labeled AVT05 as Entyvio/Stelara — it is golimumab (Simponi); AVT16 is the Entyvio program. Score held at 6 — real near-term manufacturing-overhang resolution, but the headline near-term gate is a six-month-review acceptance (no priced surprise yet) and AVT16 is a longer 2027 gate, with the mislabel docking precision.
verified.analysis: All three milestones verified from primary sources: AVT16 (Entyvio) BLA accepted Jun-8-2026 — the first vedolizumab biosimilar accepted by FDA — and AVT05 (Simponi)/AVT06 (Eylea) BLAs resubmitted Jun-4-2026 on a six-month review after Alvotech addressed the Reykjavik facility Post-Action Letter and cGMP inspection observations, pointing to a ~Dec-2026 action. The near-term, un-fired catalyst is the AVT05/AVT06 six-month review resolving the manufacturing overhang; AVT16 is a longer-dated 2027 gate. Note: triage mislabeled AVT05 as Entyvio/Stelara — it is golimumab (Simponi); the dated-binary thesis otherwise holds.

verified.catalyst: Pivotal Phase 3 LUCIDITY topline for avexitide (first-in-class GLP-1 receptor antagonist, FDA Breakthrough Therapy) in post-bariatric hypoglycemia.
verified.primary_source: Amylyx Q1 2026 Financial Results press release, May 7, 2026 (investors.amylyx.com / SEC 8-K) — "topline data readout from Phase 3 LUCIDITY ... on track for Q3 2026"; corroborated by enrollment-completion release (78 participants, Mar 2026).
verified.kill_fact: Amylyx Q1 2026 results (May 7, 2026; mirrors SEC 8-K) confirm verbatim: LUCIDITY enrollment completed March 2026 (78 pts), topline "on track and anticipated in the third quarter of 2026" on the "FDA-agreed upon primary outcome of reduction in the composite of Level 2 and Level 3 hypoglycemic events through Week 16"; avexitide is first-in-class GLP-1 receptor antagonist with FDA Breakthrough Therapy in PBH; commercial launch 2027 if approved. Forward, not fired as of 2026-06-29; dated, unpriced first-in-class approval-pathway binary in an indication with no approved drug.
verified.analysis: AMLX confirms a genuine dated, forward, unpriced clinical binary: LUCIDITY enrollment completed March 2026 (78 pts), with topline on a defined FDA-agreed primary endpoint (composite Level 2+3 hypoglycemic-event reduction through Week 16) reaffirmed "on track for Q3 2026" in the May 7, 2026 Q1 results. Avexitide is first-in-class with Breakthrough Therapy designation in PBH, an indication with no approved drug, so a positive readout de-risks the full 2027 commercial path — a clean catalyst, not a macro bet, and not yet fired. Edge is Medium rather than High because the Q3-2026 date is well-telegraphed and the binary is at least partly anticipated by the market; G3 satisfied (unpriced first-in-class approval-pathway value), G1/G2 N/A for a trial readout.
